event: plane between descemet membrane and deepest stroma
donor button placed over recipient DM
donor button sutured with recipient rim
graft removed and replaced by another stromal graft
graft and corneal scrapings sent for microbiology
topical vancomycin (50 mg/ml) and ceftazidime (100 mg/ml) started
Gram-stain showed presence of Gram-negative Bacilli
next day of repeat-deep anterior lamellar keratoplasty (reDALK)
clinical picture worse with infiltrates along entire graft host junction
topical antibiotics increased to half hourly
corneal scrapings and corneal button revealed Klebsiella pneumoniae
resistant to cefazolin, amikacin, vancomycin, ceftazidime, ofloxacin, ciprofloxacin, moxifloxacin, cefixime, chloramphenicol, tetracycline, gentamicin, and cefalexin
sensitive to imipenem, tigecycline, and partially sensitive to gatifloxacin
infiltration extended toward center of graft and hypopyon persisted
four days later, large diameter (9.5 mm donor, 9 mm recipient) therapeutic penetrating keratoplasty (TPK) carried out
infiltrates observed also in host DM
graft clear without infiltrates or hypopyon next day
gatifloxacin drops added every half hourly
prednisolone drops 1% 4 times a day added
6 weeks, unaided vision 6/60 improving to 6/18 with pin hole
graft clear, anterior segment quiet with normal intraocular pressure
